articles.headline: Murshidabad Rocked by Violence Over Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025; Three Killed
articles.explainer:
• Three individuals were killed and several injured in clashes that erupted in Murshidabad, West Bengal.
• The violence was a direct protest against the recently enacted Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025.
• Local administration has imposed Section 144 in affected areas to restore law and order.
• Political leaders and community representatives have appealed for calm and dialogue.
• The Act reportedly introduces significant changes to the administration and management of Waqf properties.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Vulnerable sections of the population
Law and order and internal security
prelimsPoints: The Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025: key provisions and changes.
Geographical location of Murshidabad in West Bengal.
Constitutional provisions related to religious endowments and minority rights.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025 on community management and property rights.
Discuss the challenges of maintaining law and order during protests against government legislation.
Evaluate the role of state and non-state actors in mediating such conflicts and ensuring social cohesion.
Examine the socio-economic factors contributing to unrest in regions like Murshidabad.
articles.imageDescription: Police personnel patrolling a street in Murshidabad amidst protests.
articles.trivia: Waqf is an endowment made by a Muslim person or a group of people for religious, educational, or charitable purposes, often a building or plot of land.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Fundamental Rights, Directive Principles, Minority Rights

articles.headline: Renowned Rai Folk Dancer Ram Sahay Panday Passes Away at 92
articles.explainer:
• Padma Shri awardee Ram Sahay Panday, a towering figure in Indian folk dance, passed away at the age of 92.
• He was celebrated for his immense contribution to the preservation and promotion of the traditional Rai folk dance of the Bundelkhand region.
• Panday dedicated his life to teaching and performing Rai, bringing it national and international recognition.
• His demise marks the end of an era for the vibrant folk traditions of Madhya Pradesh.
• He was instrumental in adapting the dance form to contemporary stages while retaining its authenticity.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Ram Sahay Panday: associated with which dance form and region?
Rai folk dance: characteristics and origin.
Padma Shri award: significance and year of award to Panday (hypothetical, assuming recent award).
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of preserving traditional folk art forms in modern India.
Analyze the role of individual artists like Ram Sahay Panday in cultural revival and promotion.
Evaluate government initiatives and challenges in supporting folk artists and cultural heritage.
articles.imageDescription: A black and white photo of Ram Sahay Panday performing the Rai dance.
articles.trivia: Rai dance is often performed by the Bediya community in Madhya Pradesh and is known for its energetic movements and vibrant costumes.

articles.headline: ISRO Provides Update on Chandrayaan-4 Mission Preparations
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced that preparations for the Chandrayaan-4 mission are progressing ahead of schedule.
• Chandrayaan-4 aims to demonstrate advanced lunar sample return capabilities, building on previous Chandrayaan missions.
• The mission is envisioned as a collaboration, potentially involving international partners for specific payload contributions.
• Scientists are focusing on the selection of a new landing site in the lunar south pole region for resource prospecting.
• A key technological focus is on developing a robust robotic arm for sample collection and a re-entry capsule for Earth return.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: Objectives of Chandrayaan-4 mission.
Previous Chandrayaan missions (1, 2, 3) and their key achievements.
Significance of lunar south pole for future missions.
Primary components of a sample return mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's growing capabilities in space exploration and its implications for global space leadership.
Analyze the scientific and economic benefits of lunar sample return missions.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in international collaboration for space projects.
Evaluate the role of space technology in national development and strategic autonomy.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Chandrayaan-4 lander collecting lunar samples.
articles.trivia: The lunar south pole is of particular interest to space agencies due to the potential presence of water ice in permanently shadowed craters.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach to tame persistent inflationary pressures, especially in food and energy sectors.
• RBI Governor stated that the focus remains on withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
• Economic growth projections were maintained, indicating a resilient domestic demand despite global headwinds.
• Experts anticipate that the RBI will continue to monitor global commodity prices and monsoon performance for future policy actions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Current Repo Rate and its significance.
Composition and function of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Tools of monetary policy (Repo, Reverse Repo, CRR, SLR).
Inflation target mandate of RBI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on interest rates.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth.
Evaluate the impact of global economic factors on India's monetary policy decisions.
Examine the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflation.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in India in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in deciding monetary policy.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds States' Rights in Inter-State River Dispute Resolution
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment, reaffirming the constitutional powers of states in inter-state river water dispute resolution.
• The ruling came in a long-standing dispute between two southern states regarding water sharing and dam construction.
• The Court emphasized the principles of cooperative federalism and equitable distribution of natural resources.
• It also clarified the jurisdiction of the Inter-State Water Disputes Act, 1956, and tribunals formed under it.
• The decision is expected to set a precedent for future water-sharing conflicts and promote dialogue over confrontation.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Dispute Redressal Mechanisms and Institutions.
prelimsPoints: Constitutional provisions related to inter-state water disputes (Article 262).
Inter-State Water Disputes Act, 1956: key features.
Role of the Supreme Court in federal disputes.
Concept of cooperative federalism.
mainsPoints: Analyze the Supreme Court's role as an arbiter in federal disputes, particularly concerning natural resources.
Discuss the challenges in resolving inter-state river water disputes in India and suggest potential solutions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing institutional mechanisms for water dispute resolution.
Examine how such rulings reinforce or challenge the principles of cooperative and competitive federalism.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
articles.trivia: The first Inter-State Water Disputes Act was enacted in 1956, allowing the Central Government to set up ad hoc tribunals to adjudicate disputes.

articles.headline: India and Japan Hold High-Level Strategic Dialogue, Focus on Indo-Pacific Security
articles.explainer:
• Senior officials from India and Japan convened for their annual Strategic Dialogue in New Delhi.
• Discussions primarily focused on strengthening bilateral security cooperation and regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and rules-based international order.
• Key areas of cooperation included defense exercises, maritime security, and counter-terrorism efforts.
• The dialogue also touched upon economic collaboration, particularly in infrastructure development and emerging technologies.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Key pillars of India-Japan strategic partnership.
Significance of the Indo-Pacific region for both countries.
Major defense exercises between India and Japan (e.g., Dharma Guardian, JIMEX).
Concept of Quad and its members.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic convergence between India and Japan in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the role of India-Japan partnership in maintaining regional stability and counterbalancing rising geopolitical challenges.
Evaluate the potential for enhanced cooperation in areas like defense technology, infrastructure, and climate change.
Examine how bilateral dialogues contribute to India's broader foreign policy objectives.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: India and Japan upgraded their relationship to 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' in 2014.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress of National Green Hydrogen Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy held a high-level meeting to review the implementation progress of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030.
• Focus areas include developing pilot projects, R&D in electrolysis technology, and creating a robust regulatory framework.
• The government highlighted the importance of private sector investment and international collaborations for achieving mission targets.
• Discussions also covered incentives for green hydrogen production and infrastructure development for its transportation and storage.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Difference between green, blue, and grey hydrogen.
Process of electrolysis for hydrogen production.
India's targets for renewable energy and green hydrogen production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private sector investment in driving the green hydrogen economy.
Examine the potential impact of green hydrogen on various industrial sectors and job creation.
articles.imageDescription: A conceptual image showing green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, generating no greenhouse gas emissions in the process.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Report on Leveraging Digital Public Infrastructure for Inclusive Growth
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog published a comprehensive report titled 'Digital Public Infrastructure: A Catalyst for Inclusive Growth in India'.
• The report highlights the transformative potential of India Stack components like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
• It recommends policy measures to further expand DPI adoption in sectors such as healthcare, education, and agriculture.
• The report also emphasizes the need for robust data governance frameworks and cybersecurity measures.
• It positions India's DPI model as a blueprint for other developing nations to achieve digital transformation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: Components of India Stack.
Role of NITI Aayog in policy formulation.
Examples of Digital Public Infrastructure.
Benefits of UPI and Aadhaar for financial inclusion.
mainsPoints: Discuss how Digital Public Infrastructure can be a game-changer for achieving inclusive growth in India.
Analyze the challenges in universalizing DPI access and ensuring digital literacy across diverse populations.
Evaluate the ethical and privacy concerns associated with large-scale digital data collection and usage.
Examine India's role in promoting its DPI model globally and its implications for digital cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic representation of interconnected digital services like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
articles.trivia: India's UPI system processes more transactions than credit cards and debit cards combined in several countries.

articles.headline: ICAR Develops New Climate-Resilient Crop Varieties for Drought-Prone Regions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) announced the development of several new climate-resilient crop varieties.
• These varieties are specifically engineered to withstand prolonged droughts and extreme weather conditions.
• The focus is on staple crops like rice, wheat, and millets, crucial for food security in rain-fed and drought-prone areas.
• Research utilized advanced breeding techniques and genetic modification to enhance water-use efficiency and heat tolerance.
• The new varieties are expected to be rolled out to farmers after successful field trials, boosting agricultural productivity and farmer incomes.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Role and functions of ICAR.
Key characteristics of climate-resilient crops.
Importance of millets in Indian agriculture.
Government initiatives for promoting drought-resistant farming.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of developing climate-resilient crop varieties for India's food security and agricultural sustainability.
Analyze the challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and the strategies to mitigate them.
Evaluate the role of agricultural research and technological innovation in enhancing farmer resilience and income.
Examine the ethical and environmental considerations associated with genetically modified crops.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers inspecting a field of drought-resistant crop variety.
articles.trivia: India is one of the largest producers of millets, which are often called 'nutri-cereals' due to their high nutritional value and ability to grow in arid conditions.

articles.headline: India-Pakistan Permanent Indus Commission Concludes Annual Meeting
articles.explainer:
• The Permanent Indus Commission, comprising water commissioners from India and Pakistan, concluded its annual meeting.
• The talks focused on technical aspects of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), including water flow data and proposed projects.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to implementing the provisions of the IWT in good faith.
• Discussions aimed at resolving minor technical disagreements and ensuring smooth information exchange.
• The meeting is seen as a crucial channel for dialogue amidst broader bilateral tensions.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Key provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty.
Rivers covered under IWT (Eastern and Western rivers).
Role of the Permanent Indus Commission.
Year of signing of IWT and mediating body.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty as a model for transboundary water management despite geopolitical tensions.
Analyze the challenges faced by India and Pakistan in adhering to the IWT and maintaining water cooperation.
Evaluate the role of international mediation in resolving water disputes between riparian states.
Examine the potential impacts of climate change on the Indus Basin and future implications for the IWT.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River System and its tributaries.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty, signed in 1960, was brokered by the World Bank and is one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world.

articles.headline: Ministry of HUPA Highlights Successes of National Urban Livelihoods Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Poverty Alleviation (HUPA) released a report showcasing the achievements of the National Urban Livelihoods Mission (NULM).
• The mission has successfully supported over 5 million urban poor through skill development, self-employment, and affordable housing initiatives.
• Particular success was noted in empowering urban women through self-help groups (SHGs) and micro-enterprises.
• The report emphasized the mission's role in creating sustainable livelihoods and reducing urban poverty.
• Future plans include expanding reach to more tier-2 and tier-3 cities and integrating with digital platforms for better outreach.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Poverty Alleviation
prelimsPoints: Objectives and key components of NULM.
Role of SHGs in empowering urban poor.
Ministry responsible for NULM.
Government initiatives for urban employment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the effectiveness of government-led livelihood missions in addressing urban poverty and unemployment.
Analyze the role of skill development and micro-enterprises in creating sustainable livelihoods for the urban poor.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing welfare schemes for the urban informal sector.
Examine how NULM contributes to India's broader inclusive growth agenda.
articles.imageDescription: Women from an urban self-help group showcasing their handicrafts.
articles.trivia: The National Urban Livelihoods Mission (NULM) was launched in 2013, replacing the Swarna Jayanti Shahari Rozgar Yojana (SJSRY).

articles.headline: CERT-In Issues New Guidelines for Enhanced Cybersecurity Framework
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) released updated guidelines for cybersecurity practices across government and critical infrastructure entities.
• The new framework emphasizes proactive threat intelligence, incident response protocols, and data protection measures.
• It mandates periodic security audits and mandatory reporting of cyber incidents within a specified timeframe.
• The guidelines aim to strengthen India's digital defenses against sophisticated cyberattacks and data breaches.
• Training and capacity building for cybersecurity professionals are also a key focus of the updated framework.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Cyber security basics; money laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: Role and mandate of CERT-In.
Key provisions of the new cybersecurity guidelines.
Importance of data protection in the digital age.
Examples of critical infrastructure sectors.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cybersecurity threats and their implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the effectiveness of regulatory frameworks like CERT-In's guidelines in enhancing India's cyber resilience.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing comprehensive cybersecurity measures across diverse public and private sectors.
Examine the need for international cooperation in combating transboundary cybercrime.
articles.imageDescription: A digital representation of a secure network with a padlock icon.
articles.trivia: CERT-In operates under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) and is the national agency for incident response.

articles.headline: Progress Report on National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Released
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released its annual progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlighted significant strides in curriculum reforms, teacher training, and integration of vocational education.
• Key achievements include the establishment of new multidisciplinary institutions and improved Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education.
• Challenges remain in ensuring equitable access to digital learning resources and bridging the learning gap in foundational literacy.
• The Ministry outlined future strategies focusing on technology integration, skill development, and internationalization of education.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Key pillars and objectives of NEP 2020.
Concept of GER and its significance.
Major changes proposed for school and higher education under NEP.
Importance of vocational education.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in revamping India's education system to meet 21st-century demands.
Discuss the challenges in implementing large-scale education reforms across a diverse country like India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of NEP in addressing issues of access, equity, quality, and accountability in education.
Examine the role of technology and teacher training in achieving the goals of NEP 2020.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting with digital learning tools.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the thirty-four-year-old National Policy on Education (NPE), 1986.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Major Milestone in Health ID Creation
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced that it has crossed 500 million Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) IDs.
• This milestone signifies significant progress in creating a seamless online platform for health records and services.
• ABHA IDs enable individuals to access and share their health records digitally across various healthcare providers.
• The mission aims to digitize the entire healthcare ecosystem, improving accessibility, efficiency, and transparency.
• Future plans include integrating more public and private healthcare facilities and expanding teleconsultation services.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Objectives and features of Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
Purpose of ABHA ID.
Role of National Health Authority (NHA).
Components of Ayushman Bharat scheme.
mainsPoints: Discuss how the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission can transform India's healthcare delivery system.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of digitizing health records and services on a national scale.
Evaluate the potential of digital health initiatives in improving healthcare access and affordability for all citizens.
Examine the privacy and data security concerns associated with a centralized digital health ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing a smartphone with a health app and the ABHA logo.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission was launched nationwide in September 2021 by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Shows Strong Revival, Focus on Sustainable Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism reported a significant rebound in both domestic and international tourist arrivals.
• The revival is attributed to relaxed travel restrictions, robust vaccination drives, and targeted promotional campaigns.
• The government is now shifting focus towards promoting sustainable and responsible tourism practices.
• Initiatives include developing eco-tourism circuits, promoting local culture, and minimizing environmental impact.
• The tourism sector is identified as a key driver for economic growth and employment generation, especially in rural areas.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Government schemes for tourism promotion (e.g., Swadesh Darshan, PRASAD).
Concept of sustainable tourism.
Contribution of tourism to India's GDP and employment.
Major tourist circuits in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of the tourism sector as a catalyst for economic growth and job creation in India.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in promoting sustainable and responsible tourism practices across diverse regions.
Evaluate the role of government policies and public-private partnerships in boosting India's tourism industry.
Examine how tourism can contribute to cultural preservation and local community development.
articles.imageDescription: Tourists visiting a historical monument in India.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top 20 most visited countries in the world, with a rich diversity of cultural, historical, and natural attractions.

articles.headline: India's 45th Scientific Expedition to Antarctica Returns with Key Climate Data
articles.explainer:
• The Indian scientific expedition to Antarctica, the 45th in the series, successfully concluded its mission and returned to India.
• Researchers collected crucial data on climate change, oceanography, geology, and atmospheric sciences.
• Special focus was on studying the impact of melting glaciers on global sea levels and biodiversity.
• The expedition utilized India's research stations, Maitri and Bharati, for year-round scientific activities.
• The findings are expected to contribute significantly to global climate models and polar research.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
articles.source: National Centre for Polar and Ocean Research (NCPOR)
prelimsPoints: Names of India's research stations in Antarctica and Arctic.
Objectives of India's Antarctic expeditions.
Role of NCPOR.
Significance of polar research for global climate studies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic and scientific importance of India's polar research program.
Analyze the contributions of Indian Antarctic expeditions to understanding global climate change and environmental processes.
Evaluate the challenges and logistical complexities involved in conducting research in extreme polar environments.
Examine India's role in international cooperation for Antarctic governance and scientific exploration.
articles.imageDescription: Indian scientists working at the Maitri research station in Antarctica.
articles.trivia: India launched its first scientific expedition to Antarctica in 1981, establishing Dakshin Gangotri, its first permanent research station, in 1983.

articles.headline: Smart Cities Mission: Significant Progress in Urban Infrastructure Projects
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced that over 70% of projects under the Smart Cities Mission have been completed.
• Major achievements include integrated command and control centers, smart mobility solutions, and urban green spaces.
• The mission has leveraged technology for efficient urban governance, waste management, and public safety.
• Challenges persist in citizen participation, inter-agency coordination, and financial sustainability of projects.
• The government aims to complete all identified projects by the end of 2026, focusing on impact assessment and scalability.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the Smart Cities Mission.
Key components of a 'Smart City'.
Ministry responsible for the mission.
Examples of smart city initiatives in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the vision and achievements of the Smart Cities Mission in transforming India's urban landscape.
Analyze the challenges encountered in the implementation of smart city projects, particularly in terms of funding and citizen engagement.
Evaluate the effectiveness of technology-driven solutions in addressing urban problems like traffic, waste management, and public safety.
Examine how the Smart Cities Mission contributes to India's broader sustainable development goals.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a modern smart city with green spaces and advanced infrastructure.
articles.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission was launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in 2015 with a target to develop 100 smart cities across India.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Initiative to Promote Women in STEM Fields
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology (DST) launched a new national initiative aimed at increasing the participation of women in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) fields.
• The program includes scholarships, mentorship opportunities, and funding for research projects led by women scientists.
• It seeks to address the gender gap in STEM careers and leadership positions.
• Special emphasis is placed on encouraging young girls to pursue science education from an early age.
• The initiative also promotes flexible working arrangements and childcare support for women researchers.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Government initiatives for women's empowerment.
Importance of STEM education for national development.
Role of DST in promoting science and technology.
Challenges faced by women in STEM careers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of increasing women's participation in STEM fields for India's scientific and economic progress.
Analyze the socio-cultural and institutional barriers that hinder women's entry and retention in STEM careers.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives and policy interventions in promoting gender equality in science.
Examine the broader impact of women's empowerment on national development and innovation.
articles.imageDescription: A group of diverse women scientists working in a laboratory.
articles.trivia: India's first woman scientist to head a major scientific institution was Dr. Aditi Pant, a marine biologist who was part of the third Indian expedition to Antarctica.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Role of Women in Society and Economy

articles.headline: India Hosts Global Health Summit on Pandemic Preparedness
articles.explainer:
• India hosted a major Global Health Summit, bringing together leaders, scientists, and policymakers from around the world.
• The summit's primary agenda was to discuss strategies for enhancing global pandemic preparedness and response mechanisms.
• Key topics included equitable vaccine access, strengthening healthcare infrastructure, and international cooperation in health research.
• India showcased its 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative and its capabilities in pharmaceutical manufacturing.
• A joint declaration was issued, emphasizing the need for a unified global approach to future health crises.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Concept of 'Global Health Security'.
India's role in global health initiatives.
Objectives of 'Vaccine Maitri'.
International organizations involved in global health (WHO, GAVI).
mainsPoints: Discuss India's emerging role as a global leader in public health and pharmaceutical manufacturing.
Analyze the lessons learned from recent pandemics and the imperative for enhanced global preparedness.
Evaluate the challenges in ensuring equitable access to healthcare resources and technologies across nations.
Examine the 'One Health' approach and its significance in preventing future zoonotic outbreaks.
articles.imageDescription: A conference hall with delegates from various countries attending a summit.
articles.trivia: India is often referred to as the 'pharmacy of the world' due to its significant contribution to global vaccine and generic drug production.

articles.headline: Construction Begins for National Maritime Heritage Complex in Lothal
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture announced the commencement of construction for the National Maritime Heritage Complex at Lothal, Gujarat.
• The complex aims to showcase India's rich maritime history from the Harappan era to modern times.
• It will feature a maritime museum, lighthouse museum, and an immersive theme park.
• Lothal, an ancient Harappan port city, was chosen for its historical significance as a major trading hub.
• The project is envisioned as a world-class tourism destination and a research hub for maritime archaeology.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Location and significance of Lothal.
Key features of the National Maritime Heritage Complex.
Contribution of Harappan civilization to maritime trade.
Other important Harappan sites.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's maritime heritage and its contribution to global trade and cultural exchange.
Analyze the role of such heritage complexes in promoting tourism, education, and national pride.
Evaluate the challenges in preserving and showcasing ancient archaeological sites like Lothal.
Examine how the project integrates historical preservation with modern tourism and research initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: An artist's impression of the National Maritime Heritage Complex at Lothal.
articles.trivia: Lothal is believed to be one of the earliest known dockyards in the world, dating back to 2400 BCE.

highlights: Murshidabad rocked by violence over new Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025; three killed in clashes.
Renowned Rai folk dancer and Padma Shri awardee Ram Sahay Panday passes away at 92.
ISRO provides positive update on Chandrayaan-4 mission preparations for lunar sample return.
RBI's Monetary Policy Committee maintains status quo on repo rate amidst persistent inflation concerns.
Supreme Court upholds states' rights in a landmark judgment on inter-state river dispute resolution.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. The Act aims to streamline the administration and management of Waqf properties.
2. Protests against the Act recently led to violence in Murshidabad, West Bengal.
3. Waqf properties are endowments made by any religious community for charitable purposes.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the Act introduces significant changes to administration and management. Statement 2 is correct as per the news. Statement 3 is incorrect because Waqf is specifically an endowment made by a Muslim person or a group of people for religious, educational, or charitable purposes, not any religious community. Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Rai folk dance':
1. It is a traditional folk dance primarily associated with the Bundelkhand region of Madhya Pradesh.
2. Padma Shri Ram Sahay Panday was a renowned exponent of this dance form.
3. The dance is typically performed during harvest festivals by the Naga community.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 and 2 are correct as per the news about Ram Sahay Panday. Statement 3 is incorrect; Rai dance is often performed by the Bediya community and is not specifically linked to harvest festivals or the Naga community.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) often referred to as 'India Stack'?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are core components of India Stack, representing the identity, payments, and data exchange layers respectively. GSTN is a non-profit, non-government company responsible for managing the IT system for the Goods and Services Tax (GST) in India, but it is not typically categorized as a core component of the 'India Stack' in the same manner as the other three.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's polar research, consider the following statements:
1. India has two operational research stations in Antarctica: Maitri and Bharati.
2. The National Centre for Polar and Ocean Research (NCPOR) is the nodal agency for India's polar expeditions.
3. India's first permanent research station in Antarctica was Dakshin Gangotri.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. India operates Maitri and Bharati in Antarctica, NCPOR manages the program, and Dakshin Gangotri was indeed the first permanent station, though it is now largely decommissioned as a primary research base.

prelimsMocks.question:
The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) primarily governs the sharing of waters of which of the following groups of rivers between India and Pakistan?
(a) Ganga, Yamuna, Brahmaputra
(b) Indus, Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, Beas, Sutlej
(c) Narmada, Tapi, Godavari
(d) Krishna, Cauvery, Periyar
options: (b) Indus, Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, Beas, Sutlej
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Indus, Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, Beas, Sutlej
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) specifically allocates the waters of the Indus system of rivers. The 'Western Rivers' (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) are largely for Pakistan, while the 'Eastern Rivers' (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) are for India. Option (b) lists all these rivers.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the implications of the Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025, on community rights and property management. Discuss the challenges faced by the government in ensuring social harmony while implementing such legislative changes.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population; Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025 and its context (violence in Murshidabad). Discuss the potential implications on Waqf properties, community autonomy, and minority rights. Analyze the challenges for the government, including maintaining law and order, addressing grievances, ensuring equitable implementation, and fostering dialogue. Conclude with measures for inclusive governance.

mainsMocks.question: India's space program is increasingly focusing on advanced lunar missions like Chandrayaan-4. Discuss the strategic and scientific significance of lunar sample return missions. What are the key technological challenges India needs to overcome to achieve its ambitious lunar exploration goals?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Achievements of Indians in science & technology; Indigenization of technology and developing new technology; Space technology.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by highlighting Chandrayaan-4 and India's lunar ambitions. Elaborate on the scientific benefits (understanding lunar geology, origin of solar system, resource potential) and strategic importance (technological prowess, international collaboration, geopolitical standing) of sample return missions. Discuss technological challenges like advanced robotics, precise landing, sample preservation, and re-entry systems. Conclude with India's capabilities and future roadmap.

mainsMocks.question: The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen. Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change commitments. What are the major challenges in scaling up its production and adoption in the country?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its objectives. Explain the strategic importance of green hydrogen for energy independence, reducing fossil fuel imports, and achieving net-zero targets. Detail the challenges, including high production costs, need for massive renewable energy infrastructure, water availability, storage and transportation issues, and regulatory hurdles. Suggest policy measures and technological advancements to overcome these challenges.

quickBytes.text: The Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025, has sparked protests, leading to violence in Murshidabad.
Ram Sahay Panday, a Padma Shri awardee, was a master of the Rai folk dance from Bundelkhand.
ISRO's Chandrayaan-4 mission aims for advanced lunar sample return capabilities.
The RBI's MPC kept the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% to combat persistent inflation.
Article 262 of the Indian Constitution deals with the adjudication of inter-state river water disputes.
India and Japan's Strategic Dialogue focused on Indo-Pacific security and bilateral cooperation.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy, emitting no carbon.
Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are key components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (India Stack).
ICAR has developed new climate-resilient crop varieties for drought-prone agricultural regions.
Lothal, an ancient Harappan port city, is the site for the National Maritime Heritage Complex.

GS1.headline: Renowned Rai Folk Dancer Ram Sahay Panday Passes Away at 92
GS1.explainer:
• Padma Shri awardee Ram Sahay Panday, a towering figure in Indian folk dance, passed away at the age of 92.
• He was celebrated for his immense contribution to the preservation and promotion of the traditional Rai folk dance of the Bundelkhand region.
• Panday dedicated his life to teaching and performing Rai, bringing it national and international recognition.
• His demise marks the end of an era for the vibrant folk traditions of Madhya Pradesh.
• He was instrumental in adapting the dance form to contemporary stages while retaining its authenticity.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Ram Sahay Panday: associated with which dance form and region?
Rai folk dance: characteristics and origin.
Padma Shri award: significance and year of award to Panday (hypothetical, assuming recent award).
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of preserving traditional folk art forms in modern India.
Analyze the role of individual artists like Ram Sahay Panday in cultural revival and promotion.
Evaluate government initiatives and challenges in supporting folk artists and cultural heritage.
GS1.imageDescription: A black and white photo of Ram Sahay Panday performing the Rai dance.
GS1.trivia: Rai dance is often performed by the Bediya community in Madhya Pradesh and is known for its energetic movements and vibrant costumes.

GS1.headline: Government Launches New Initiative to Promote Women in STEM Fields
GS1.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology (DST) launched a new national initiative aimed at increasing the participation of women in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) fields.
• The program includes scholarships, mentorship opportunities, and funding for research projects led by women scientists.
• It seeks to address the gender gap in STEM careers and leadership positions.
• Special emphasis is placed on encouraging young girls to pursue science education from an early age.
• The initiative also promotes flexible working arrangements and childcare support for women researchers.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Government initiatives for women's empowerment.
Importance of STEM education for national development.
Role of DST in promoting science and technology.
Challenges faced by women in STEM careers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of increasing women's participation in STEM fields for India's scientific and economic progress.
Analyze the socio-cultural and institutional barriers that hinder women's entry and retention in STEM careers.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives and policy interventions in promoting gender equality in science.
Examine the broader impact of women's empowerment on national development and innovation.
GS1.imageDescription: A group of diverse women scientists working in a laboratory.
GS1.trivia: India's first woman scientist to head a major scientific institution was Dr. Aditi Pant, a marine biologist who was part of the third Indian expedition to Antarctica.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Role of Women in Society and Economy

GS1.headline: Construction Begins for National Maritime Heritage Complex in Lothal
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture announced the commencement of construction for the National Maritime Heritage Complex at Lothal, Gujarat.
• The complex aims to showcase India's rich maritime history from the Harappan era to modern times.
• It will feature a maritime museum, lighthouse museum, and an immersive theme park.
• Lothal, an ancient Harappan port city, was chosen for its historical significance as a major trading hub.
• The project is envisioned as a world-class tourism destination and a research hub for maritime archaeology.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Location and significance of Lothal.
Key features of the National Maritime Heritage Complex.
Contribution of Harappan civilization to maritime trade.
Other important Harappan sites.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's maritime heritage and its contribution to global trade and cultural exchange.
Analyze the role of such heritage complexes in promoting tourism, education, and national pride.
Evaluate the challenges in preserving and showcasing ancient archaeological sites like Lothal.
Examine how the project integrates historical preservation with modern tourism and research initiatives.
GS1.imageDescription: An artist's impression of the National Maritime Heritage Complex at Lothal.
GS1.trivia: Lothal is believed to be one of the earliest known dockyards in the world, dating back to 2400 BCE.

GS2.headline: Murshidabad Rocked by Violence Over Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025; Three Killed
GS2.explainer:
• Three individuals were killed and several injured in clashes that erupted in Murshidabad, West Bengal.
• The violence was a direct protest against the recently enacted Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025.
• Local administration has imposed Section 144 in affected areas to restore law and order.
• Political leaders and community representatives have appealed for calm and dialogue.
• The Act reportedly introduces significant changes to the administration and management of Waqf properties.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Vulnerable sections of the population
Law and order and internal security
prelimsPoints: The Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025: key provisions and changes.
Geographical location of Murshidabad in West Bengal.
Constitutional provisions related to religious endowments and minority rights.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025 on community management and property rights.
Discuss the challenges of maintaining law and order during protests against government legislation.
Evaluate the role of state and non-state actors in mediating such conflicts and ensuring social cohesion.
Examine the socio-economic factors contributing to unrest in regions like Murshidabad.
GS2.imageDescription: Police personnel patrolling a street in Murshidabad amidst protests.
GS2.trivia: Waqf is an endowment made by a Muslim person or a group of people for religious, educational, or charitable purposes, often a building or plot of land.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Fundamental Rights, Directive Principles, Minority Rights

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds States' Rights in Inter-State River Dispute Resolution
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment, reaffirming the constitutional powers of states in inter-state river water dispute resolution.
• The ruling came in a long-standing dispute between two southern states regarding water sharing and dam construction.
• The Court emphasized the principles of cooperative federalism and equitable distribution of natural resources.
• It also clarified the jurisdiction of the Inter-State Water Disputes Act, 1956, and tribunals formed under it.
• The decision is expected to set a precedent for future water-sharing conflicts and promote dialogue over confrontation.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Dispute Redressal Mechanisms and Institutions.
prelimsPoints: Constitutional provisions related to inter-state water disputes (Article 262).
Inter-State Water Disputes Act, 1956: key features.
Role of the Supreme Court in federal disputes.
Concept of cooperative federalism.
mainsPoints: Analyze the Supreme Court's role as an arbiter in federal disputes, particularly concerning natural resources.
Discuss the challenges in resolving inter-state river water disputes in India and suggest potential solutions.
Evaluate the effectiveness of existing institutional mechanisms for water dispute resolution.
Examine how such rulings reinforce or challenge the principles of cooperative and competitive federalism.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
GS2.trivia: The first Inter-State Water Disputes Act was enacted in 1956, allowing the Central Government to set up ad hoc tribunals to adjudicate disputes.

GS2.headline: India and Japan Hold High-Level Strategic Dialogue, Focus on Indo-Pacific Security
GS2.explainer:
• Senior officials from India and Japan convened for their annual Strategic Dialogue in New Delhi.
• Discussions primarily focused on strengthening bilateral security cooperation and regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and rules-based international order.
• Key areas of cooperation included defense exercises, maritime security, and counter-terrorism efforts.
• The dialogue also touched upon economic collaboration, particularly in infrastructure development and emerging technologies.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: Key pillars of India-Japan strategic partnership.
Significance of the Indo-Pacific region for both countries.
Major defense exercises between India and Japan (e.g., Dharma Guardian, JIMEX).
Concept of Quad and its members.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic convergence between India and Japan in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the role of India-Japan partnership in maintaining regional stability and counterbalancing rising geopolitical challenges.
Evaluate the potential for enhanced cooperation in areas like defense technology, infrastructure, and climate change.
Examine how bilateral dialogues contribute to India's broader foreign policy objectives.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: India and Japan upgraded their relationship to 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' in 2014.

GS2.headline: India-Pakistan Permanent Indus Commission Concludes Annual Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• The Permanent Indus Commission, comprising water commissioners from India and Pakistan, concluded its annual meeting.
• The talks focused on technical aspects of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), including water flow data and proposed projects.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to implementing the provisions of the IWT in good faith.
• Discussions aimed at resolving minor technical disagreements and ensuring smooth information exchange.
• The meeting is seen as a crucial channel for dialogue amidst broader bilateral tensions.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: Key provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty.
Rivers covered under IWT (Eastern and Western rivers).
Role of the Permanent Indus Commission.
Year of signing of IWT and mediating body.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Indus Waters Treaty as a model for transboundary water management despite geopolitical tensions.
Analyze the challenges faced by India and Pakistan in adhering to the IWT and maintaining water cooperation.
Evaluate the role of international mediation in resolving water disputes between riparian states.
Examine the potential impacts of climate change on the Indus Basin and future implications for the IWT.
GS2.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River System and its tributaries.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty, signed in 1960, was brokered by the World Bank and is one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world.

GS2.headline: Ministry of HUPA Highlights Successes of National Urban Livelihoods Mission
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Poverty Alleviation (HUPA) released a report showcasing the achievements of the National Urban Livelihoods Mission (NULM).
• The mission has successfully supported over 5 million urban poor through skill development, self-employment, and affordable housing initiatives.
• Particular success was noted in empowering urban women through self-help groups (SHGs) and micro-enterprises.
• The report emphasized the mission's role in creating sustainable livelihoods and reducing urban poverty.
• Future plans include expanding reach to more tier-2 and tier-3 cities and integrating with digital platforms for better outreach.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
GS2.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Poverty Alleviation
prelimsPoints: Objectives and key components of NULM.
Role of SHGs in empowering urban poor.
Ministry responsible for NULM.
Government initiatives for urban employment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the effectiveness of government-led livelihood missions in addressing urban poverty and unemployment.
Analyze the role of skill development and micro-enterprises in creating sustainable livelihoods for the urban poor.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing welfare schemes for the urban informal sector.
Examine how NULM contributes to India's broader inclusive growth agenda.
GS2.imageDescription: Women from an urban self-help group showcasing their handicrafts.
GS2.trivia: The National Urban Livelihoods Mission (NULM) was launched in 2013, replacing the Swarna Jayanti Shahari Rozgar Yojana (SJSRY).

GS2.headline: Progress Report on National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Released
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released its annual progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlighted significant strides in curriculum reforms, teacher training, and integration of vocational education.
• Key achievements include the establishment of new multidisciplinary institutions and improved Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education.
• Challenges remain in ensuring equitable access to digital learning resources and bridging the learning gap in foundational literacy.
• The Ministry outlined future strategies focusing on technology integration, skill development, and internationalization of education.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Key pillars and objectives of NEP 2020.
Concept of GER and its significance.
Major changes proposed for school and higher education under NEP.
Importance of vocational education.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in revamping India's education system to meet 21st-century demands.
Discuss the challenges in implementing large-scale education reforms across a diverse country like India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of NEP in addressing issues of access, equity, quality, and accountability in education.
Examine the role of technology and teacher training in achieving the goals of NEP 2020.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom setting with digital learning tools.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the thirty-four-year-old National Policy on Education (NPE), 1986.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Major Milestone in Health ID Creation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced that it has crossed 500 million Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) IDs.
• This milestone signifies significant progress in creating a seamless online platform for health records and services.
• ABHA IDs enable individuals to access and share their health records digitally across various healthcare providers.
• The mission aims to digitize the entire healthcare ecosystem, improving accessibility, efficiency, and transparency.
• Future plans include integrating more public and private healthcare facilities and expanding teleconsultation services.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Objectives and features of Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission.
Purpose of ABHA ID.
Role of National Health Authority (NHA).
Components of Ayushman Bharat scheme.
mainsPoints: Discuss how the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission can transform India's healthcare delivery system.
Analyze the benefits and challenges of digitizing health records and services on a national scale.
Evaluate the potential of digital health initiatives in improving healthcare access and affordability for all citizens.
Examine the privacy and data security concerns associated with a centralized digital health ecosystem.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic showing a smartphone with a health app and the ABHA logo.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission was launched nationwide in September 2021 by Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

GS2.headline: Smart Cities Mission: Significant Progress in Urban Infrastructure Projects
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs announced that over 70% of projects under the Smart Cities Mission have been completed.
• Major achievements include integrated command and control centers, smart mobility solutions, and urban green spaces.
• The mission has leveraged technology for efficient urban governance, waste management, and public safety.
• Challenges persist in citizen participation, inter-agency coordination, and financial sustainability of projects.
• The government aims to complete all identified projects by the end of 2026, focusing on impact assessment and scalability.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
GS2.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the Smart Cities Mission.
Key components of a 'Smart City'.
Ministry responsible for the mission.
Examples of smart city initiatives in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the vision and achievements of the Smart Cities Mission in transforming India's urban landscape.
Analyze the challenges encountered in the implementation of smart city projects, particularly in terms of funding and citizen engagement.
Evaluate the effectiveness of technology-driven solutions in addressing urban problems like traffic, waste management, and public safety.
Examine how the Smart Cities Mission contributes to India's broader sustainable development goals.
GS2.imageDescription: An aerial view of a modern smart city with green spaces and advanced infrastructure.
GS2.trivia: The Smart Cities Mission was launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in 2015 with a target to develop 100 smart cities across India.

GS2.headline: India Hosts Global Health Summit on Pandemic Preparedness
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted a major Global Health Summit, bringing together leaders, scientists, and policymakers from around the world.
• The summit's primary agenda was to discuss strategies for enhancing global pandemic preparedness and response mechanisms.
• Key topics included equitable vaccine access, strengthening healthcare infrastructure, and international cooperation in health research.
• India showcased its 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative and its capabilities in pharmaceutical manufacturing.
• A joint declaration was issued, emphasizing the need for a unified global approach to future health crises.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Concept of 'Global Health Security'.
India's role in global health initiatives.
Objectives of 'Vaccine Maitri'.
International organizations involved in global health (WHO, GAVI).
mainsPoints: Discuss India's emerging role as a global leader in public health and pharmaceutical manufacturing.
Analyze the lessons learned from recent pandemics and the imperative for enhanced global preparedness.
Evaluate the challenges in ensuring equitable access to healthcare resources and technologies across nations.
Examine the 'One Health' approach and its significance in preventing future zoonotic outbreaks.
GS2.imageDescription: A conference hall with delegates from various countries attending a summit.
GS2.trivia: India is often referred to as the 'pharmacy of the world' due to its significant contribution to global vaccine and generic drug production.

GS3.headline: ISRO Provides Update on Chandrayaan-4 Mission Preparations
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced that preparations for the Chandrayaan-4 mission are progressing ahead of schedule.
• Chandrayaan-4 aims to demonstrate advanced lunar sample return capabilities, building on previous Chandrayaan missions.
• The mission is envisioned as a collaboration, potentially involving international partners for specific payload contributions.
• Scientists are focusing on the selection of a new landing site in the lunar south pole region for resource prospecting.
• A key technological focus is on developing a robust robotic arm for sample collection and a re-entry capsule for Earth return.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: Objectives of Chandrayaan-4 mission.
Previous Chandrayaan missions (1, 2, 3) and their key achievements.
Significance of lunar south pole for future missions.
Primary components of a sample return mission.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's growing capabilities in space exploration and its implications for global space leadership.
Analyze the scientific and economic benefits of lunar sample return missions.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in international collaboration for space projects.
Evaluate the role of space technology in national development and strategic autonomy.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Chandrayaan-4 lander collecting lunar samples.
GS3.trivia: The lunar south pole is of particular interest to space agencies due to the potential presence of water ice in permanently shadowed craters.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach to tame persistent inflationary pressures, especially in food and energy sectors.
• RBI Governor stated that the focus remains on withdrawing accommodation to ensure inflation aligns with the target.
• Economic growth projections were maintained, indicating a resilient domestic demand despite global headwinds.
• Experts anticipate that the RBI will continue to monitor global commodity prices and monsoon performance for future policy actions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: Current Repo Rate and its significance.
Composition and function of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Tools of monetary policy (Repo, Reverse Repo, CRR, SLR).
Inflation target mandate of RBI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on interest rates.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with economic growth.
Evaluate the impact of global economic factors on India's monetary policy decisions.
Examine the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflation.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in India in 2016 to bring transparency and accountability in deciding monetary policy.

GS3.headline: Government Reviews Progress of National Green Hydrogen Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy held a high-level meeting to review the implementation progress of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export by 2030.
• Focus areas include developing pilot projects, R&D in electrolysis technology, and creating a robust regulatory framework.
• The government highlighted the importance of private sector investment and international collaborations for achieving mission targets.
• Discussions also covered incentives for green hydrogen production and infrastructure development for its transportation and storage.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
Difference between green, blue, and grey hydrogen.
Process of electrolysis for hydrogen production.
India's targets for renewable energy and green hydrogen production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private sector investment in driving the green hydrogen economy.
Examine the potential impact of green hydrogen on various industrial sectors and job creation.
GS3.imageDescription: A conceptual image showing green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, generating no greenhouse gas emissions in the process.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Report on Leveraging Digital Public Infrastructure for Inclusive Growth
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog published a comprehensive report titled 'Digital Public Infrastructure: A Catalyst for Inclusive Growth in India'.
• The report highlights the transformative potential of India Stack components like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
• It recommends policy measures to further expand DPI adoption in sectors such as healthcare, education, and agriculture.
• The report also emphasizes the need for robust data governance frameworks and cybersecurity measures.
• It positions India's DPI model as a blueprint for other developing nations to achieve digital transformation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: Components of India Stack.
Role of NITI Aayog in policy formulation.
Examples of Digital Public Infrastructure.
Benefits of UPI and Aadhaar for financial inclusion.
mainsPoints: Discuss how Digital Public Infrastructure can be a game-changer for achieving inclusive growth in India.
Analyze the challenges in universalizing DPI access and ensuring digital literacy across diverse populations.
Evaluate the ethical and privacy concerns associated with large-scale digital data collection and usage.
Examine India's role in promoting its DPI model globally and its implications for digital cooperation.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic representation of interconnected digital services like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
GS3.trivia: India's UPI system processes more transactions than credit cards and debit cards combined in several countries.

GS3.headline: ICAR Develops New Climate-Resilient Crop Varieties for Drought-Prone Regions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) announced the development of several new climate-resilient crop varieties.
• These varieties are specifically engineered to withstand prolonged droughts and extreme weather conditions.
• The focus is on staple crops like rice, wheat, and millets, crucial for food security in rain-fed and drought-prone areas.
• Research utilized advanced breeding techniques and genetic modification to enhance water-use efficiency and heat tolerance.
• The new varieties are expected to be rolled out to farmers after successful field trials, boosting agricultural productivity and farmer incomes.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Role and functions of ICAR.
Key characteristics of climate-resilient crops.
Importance of millets in Indian agriculture.
Government initiatives for promoting drought-resistant farming.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of developing climate-resilient crop varieties for India's food security and agricultural sustainability.
Analyze the challenges posed by climate change to Indian agriculture and the strategies to mitigate them.
Evaluate the role of agricultural research and technological innovation in enhancing farmer resilience and income.
Examine the ethical and environmental considerations associated with genetically modified crops.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmers inspecting a field of drought-resistant crop variety.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the largest producers of millets, which are often called 'nutri-cereals' due to their high nutritional value and ability to grow in arid conditions.

GS3.headline: CERT-In Issues New Guidelines for Enhanced Cybersecurity Framework
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) released updated guidelines for cybersecurity practices across government and critical infrastructure entities.
• The new framework emphasizes proactive threat intelligence, incident response protocols, and data protection measures.
• It mandates periodic security audits and mandatory reporting of cyber incidents within a specified timeframe.
• The guidelines aim to strengthen India's digital defenses against sophisticated cyberattacks and data breaches.
• Training and capacity building for cybersecurity professionals are also a key focus of the updated framework.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Cyber security basics; money laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: Role and mandate of CERT-In.
Key provisions of the new cybersecurity guidelines.
Importance of data protection in the digital age.
Examples of critical infrastructure sectors.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cybersecurity threats and their implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the effectiveness of regulatory frameworks like CERT-In's guidelines in enhancing India's cyber resilience.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing comprehensive cybersecurity measures across diverse public and private sectors.
Examine the need for international cooperation in combating transboundary cybercrime.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital representation of a secure network with a padlock icon.
GS3.trivia: CERT-In operates under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) and is the national agency for incident response.

GS3.headline: India's Tourism Sector Shows Strong Revival, Focus on Sustainable Tourism
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism reported a significant rebound in both domestic and international tourist arrivals.
• The revival is attributed to relaxed travel restrictions, robust vaccination drives, and targeted promotional campaigns.
• The government is now shifting focus towards promoting sustainable and responsible tourism practices.
• Initiatives include developing eco-tourism circuits, promoting local culture, and minimizing environmental impact.
• The tourism sector is identified as a key driver for economic growth and employment generation, especially in rural areas.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Government schemes for tourism promotion (e.g., Swadesh Darshan, PRASAD).
Concept of sustainable tourism.
Contribution of tourism to India's GDP and employment.
Major tourist circuits in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of the tourism sector as a catalyst for economic growth and job creation in India.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in promoting sustainable and responsible tourism practices across diverse regions.
Evaluate the role of government policies and public-private partnerships in boosting India's tourism industry.
Examine how tourism can contribute to cultural preservation and local community development.
GS3.imageDescription: Tourists visiting a historical monument in India.
GS3.trivia: India ranks among the top 20 most visited countries in the world, with a rich diversity of cultural, historical, and natural attractions.

GS3.headline: India's 45th Scientific Expedition to Antarctica Returns with Key Climate Data
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian scientific expedition to Antarctica, the 45th in the series, successfully concluded its mission and returned to India.
• Researchers collected crucial data on climate change, oceanography, geology, and atmospheric sciences.
• Special focus was on studying the impact of melting glaciers on global sea levels and biodiversity.
• The expedition utilized India's research stations, Maitri and Bharati, for year-round scientific activities.
• The findings are expected to contribute significantly to global climate models and polar research.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
GS3.source: National Centre for Polar and Ocean Research (NCPOR)
prelimsPoints: Names of India's research stations in Antarctica and Arctic.
Objectives of India's Antarctic expeditions.
Role of NCPOR.
Significance of polar research for global climate studies.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic and scientific importance of India's polar research program.
Analyze the contributions of Indian Antarctic expeditions to understanding global climate change and environmental processes.
Evaluate the challenges and logistical complexities involved in conducting research in extreme polar environments.
Examine India's role in international cooperation for Antarctic governance and scientific exploration.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian scientists working at the Maitri research station in Antarctica.
GS3.trivia: India launched its first scientific expedition to Antarctica in 1981, establishing Dakshin Gangotri, its first permanent research station, in 1983.
